stories.headline: Iran launches missiles at Israel for first time since ceasefire, Israeli military says
stories.summary: The apparent attack Sunday night, Tehran’s first such strike in two months, threatened to further complicate efforts to broker a peace deal aimed at ending the war.

stories.headline: Zelensky in Downing Street for Ukraine war talks with European leaders
stories.summary: Zelensky's meeting with Ukraine's staunchest European allies comes as US President Donald Trump's focus has shifted to the war with Iran.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy meets key allies in London aiming to build on strikes on Russia
stories.summary: Keir Starmer hosts Ukrainian, French and German leaders in Downing Street amid optimism in Kyiv over state of warVolodymyr Zelenskyy met his staunchest allies at Downing Street on Sunday night, hours after a Russian drone strike damaged a storage centre for spent nuclear fuel nine miles from the Chornobyl nuclear power plant.Keir Starmer welcomed Zelenskyy, Emmanuel Macron and Friedrich Merz for a meeting to discuss ongoing support for Ukraine as

stories.headline: Russian drone hits building storing spent nuclear fuel near Chornobyl
stories.summary: Attack was ‘extremely vile’ and deliberate, says Ukraine’s president Volodymyr Zelenskyy A Russian Shahed drone has substantially damaged a building used to store spent nuclear fuel close to the disused Chornobyl nuclear power plant, in what Ukraine’s president described as a deliberate and “extremely vile” attack.While the structure – the reception building of the spent fuel storage facility – was empty of containers at the time, the targeting o

stories.headline: Iran losing patience with Israel and US over ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Iran’s patience has reached its limit with the continuous violations of the ‘ceasefire’.

stories.headline: Iran launches missiles at Israel in the first such bombardment since a ceasefire in April
stories.summary: There are no immediate reports of injuries or damage in the attack, the first by Iran on Israel since a ceasefire took effect on April 8.

stories.headline: Israel kills 13 in Gaza as Egypt hosts renewed ‘ceasefire’ talks
stories.summary: Israeli attacks came near Khan Younis, in Gaza City and Deir el-Balah as Hamas and other groups met mediators in Cairo.

stories.headline: FIFA World Cup Set to Be Most Lucrative Cup Amid US Hosting Concerns
stories.summary: The upcoming FIFA World Cup, hosted across North America, is projected to generate a record $11 billion in revenue. However, there are some questions on the actual benefits for the United States playing host. Bloomberg Business of Sports Columnist Adam Minter came on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss what hosting such an event means for the host country.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Iran's World Cup team lands in Mexico amid US visa row
stories.summary: All of Iran's group games are in the US, but players and support staff will have to fly in and out of the country on match day.

stories.headline: Iran launches ballistic missiles at Israel
stories.summary: Attack comes after Israel carried out a strike in Beirut that it said targeted Hizbollah

stories.headline: Trump Defends Compensation Fund and Iran War in ‘Meet the Press’ Interview
stories.summary: In a lengthy interview with NBC’s “Meet the Press,” the president again vowed that gas prices would go down when the war in Iran ends.

stories.headline: European leaders to meet Zelenskyy at Downing Street as pressure mounts on Russia
stories.summary: The talks will focus on coordinating European support for Ukraine, increasing pressure on Russia and efforts to achieve a just and lasting peace, according to French President Emmanuel Macron.

stories.headline: Iran’s World Cup squad lands in Mexico amid US visa row
stories.summary: Iranian delegation arrives at their World Cup base camp a day after several members were denied visas by the US.

stories.headline: Iran’s World Cup team arrives in Mexico as US visa row continues
stories.summary: Iran’s national football team has arrived in Mexico ahead of its World Cup matches in the US.

stories.headline: Iran fires missiles at Israel after it attacked Beirut
stories.summary: Videos show missiles over Israel as the Israeli military says Iran launched a new wave of attacks.

stories.summary: Former Chelsea FC owner invited to Kyiv last month in failed attempt to broker direct negotiations

stories.summary: The US defence secretary was speaking in Normandy, 82 years after allied forces launched their operation to liberate Nazi-occupied north-western Europe.

stories.headline: Russian drone hits nuclear fuel site near Chernobyl
stories.summary: Radiation levels remain ‘within normal limits’, says Ukraine’s state nuclear company

stories.headline: Trump walks out of ‘Meet the Press’ interview when challenged over false claims
stories.summary: When pressed by host Kristen Welker, the president cited no evidence for claims about Jan. 6 and elections he said were “rigged.”

stories.summary: David Shoebridge says Australia could become embroiled in a US war with China if purchase of Virginia-class attack submarines proceedsAnthony Albanese has reiterated that Aukus is “full-steam ahead” after the Greens renewed calls to cancel the nuclear-powered submarines deal, which the minor party warned could draw Australia into a potential US war with China.Debate over the security pact has resurfaced after the announcement that Australia would

stories.headline: Trump says will not unfreeze Iranian assets before ceasefire deal reached
stories.summary: Iranian officials have indicated the release of funds would build 'trust' needed to reach lasting deal ending war.

stories.headline: Trump abruptly ends NBC interview after clash over 'rigged election' claim
stories.summary: During the interview, the president was repeatedly challenged on several points by the show's presenter Kristen Welker.

stories.headline: Israel attacks Beirut days after Trump’s showdown with Netanyahu
stories.summary: Limited strikes on Lebanese capital follow US president’s intervention last week forcing Israeli PM to delay major raid

stories.headline: Trump piles pressure on Warsh with call for rate cut
stories.summary: US president raises stakes for central bank pick as expectations grow that borrowing costs will rise

stories.headline: Sen. Armstrong Advocates for Energy Infrastructure Expansion
stories.summary: Senator Alan Armstrong recently resigned as the executive chairman of Williams Companies to replace Markwayne Mullin in the Senate. Armstrong joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the need for US infrastructure to meet the growing energy demand of AI data centers.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Worries Grow That Hedge Fund Crowding May Amplify Risk in Crisis
stories.summary: Friday’s sudden equities rout after a months-long rally is renewing concerns that the unwinding of crowded trades could exacerbate market losses.

stories.headline: Lawsuit Aims to Stop U.F.C. Fights at White House on Trump’s Birthday
stories.summary: A federal lawsuit said the event, set for June 14, was unlawfully planned and designed to benefit Mr. Trump and his allies.

stories.headline: Lawsuit seeks to stop Trump’s planned White House UFC match
stories.summary: Legal challenge claims US president did not seek proper approval for fighting event to be held on his 80th birthday.

stories.headline: Will America’s evangelicals stick with Trump?

stories.headline: Embraer Arijan Meijer on Tariff Impact Within Aviation
stories.summary: Embraer CEO of Commercial Aviation Arijan Meijer speaks at the International Air Transport Association (IATA) on the impact of tariffs saying it "makes sense" for aviation to be excluded. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Stocks Face Rising Risk With Mega AI Deals Ready to Flood Market
stories.summary: A flood of new shares from companies looking to fund their artificial intelligence ambitions is raising questions on Wall Street about whether there will be enough buyers to soak them all up and what this pile of fresh equity will mean for stock prices more broadly.

stories.headline: Etihad Airways CEO on Competition & Asian Market Growth
stories.summary: Etihad Airways CEO Antonoaldo Neves speaks at the International Air Transport Association (IATA) on EU carrier advantages, growing Asian traffic, and competition. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Bloomberg This Weekend 6/07/2026
stories.summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by Florida Rep. Greg Steube, Air France-KLM Benjamin Smith, United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby, Oklahoma Sen. Alan Armstrong, GE Aerospace Larry Culp, Puck News Washington Reporter Abby Livingston and Eagleton Center for Public Interest Polling Ashley Kon

stories.headline: Brazil Budget Freeze Grounds Aircraft Approval Process
stories.summary: Brazil’s decision to freeze part of the budget of its civil aviation regulator is threatening to delay the arrival of new aircraft and planned capacity expansions just as airlines race to meet robust travel demand and address fleet shortages.

stories.headline: OPEC+ Agrees Another Symbolic Quota Increase for July
stories.summary: Major OPEC+ members agreed another modest symbolic increase to their oil output quotas for July, even as a blockage of exports from the Persian Gulf prevents most of them from implementing it.

stories.headline: Can a Forgotten Caribbean Town Become a Spaceport?
stories.summary: The Dominican Republic’s plan to build a commercial spaceport in the small town of Oviedo is about far more than rockets. Launch on Demand founder Burton Catledge says the project could create thousands of jobs, attract new investment, and help diversify the country beyond tourism. Supporters envision a new hub for aerospace, technology, and education, while local residents hope the project can bring opportunities to a region that has long felt o

stories.headline: ‘Ugly in a beautiful way’: Denmark’s mullet championship celebrates divisive hairstyle
stories.summary: Danish follicle rebels go head to head in competition for best short-in-the-front, long-in-the-back cutBusiness in the front, party in the back. A packed Danish crowd has celebrated the much-maligned but enduring mullet hairstyle, defined by very short hair at the front and longer hair at the back.Denmark’s raucous 2026 Mullet Championship, presented on an outdoor stage in central Copenhagen, attracted 12 well-coiffed competitors and more than 1,

stories.headline: Hezbollah MP: Resistance defends Lebanon where the state failed
stories.summary: Ibrahim Moussawi says Hezbollah stepped in after diplomacy and the Lebanese state failed.

stories.headline: Bolivia’s legislature passes law allowing use of troops against protesters
stories.summary: New law grants president power to use military to clear roadblocks set up amid weeks of antigovernment demonstrations.

stories.summary: Race between right-wing Fujimori and left-wing Sanchez follows first round of voting marred by logistical issues.

stories.headline: Ebola spread in central Africa could match 2014 record outbreak, US health officials say
stories.summary: Modelling from US CDC shows Ebola spread could be on ‘dangerous trajectory’, but experts warn outbreaks can be very hard to predictCentral Africa’s Ebola outbreak could spread to be similar in scale to the worst outbreak in history, west Africa’s 2014-2016 outbreak that killed more than 11,000 people, according to a new analysis by US health officials.The US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) on Friday published a range of scenarios

stories.headline: Funeral held for baby shot dead by Israeli troops in occupied West Bank
stories.summary: The seven-month-old boy's shrouded body was wrapped in a Palestinian flag and carried by his father to the graveyard.

stories.headline: ECB Steps Up as G7’s Lead Hawk With Interest-Rate Hike Primed
stories.summary: A euro-zone interest-rate hike in the coming week is set to place the European Central Bank at the vanguard of global tightening caused by the Iran war.

stories.headline: Bob Packwood, Senator Forced to Quit in Sex Scandal, Dies at 93
stories.summary: An unorthodox Republican, he helped shape tax policy and women’s rights legislation before resigning in 1995 amid accusations by more than 20 women of sexual misconduct.

stories.headline: On China, Trump picked the right battle but the wrong strategy
stories.summary: A long trade war looms. Trump’s scattershot protectionism, chaotic tariffs and belligerence against our natural allies guarantees that US trade policy will remain a hot messWe are in for a long trade war.In the months since “Liberation Day” last year, when Donald Trump let loose a volley of tariffs against imports from everywhere, countries have rushed to build new relationships in the hope of maybe circumventing the US to protect the global trad

stories.headline: Insecurity and instability drive voters in Peru's tight presidential race
stories.summary: After eight presidents in 10 years, many voters are looking for stability so the next president can focus on tackling crime and inequality.

stories.headline: Ragas and symphonies: Indian maestro Ilaiyaraaja is still reshaping music 50 years on
stories.summary: Fifty years after his debut, Ilaiyaraaja remains one of the most celebrated composers in Indian cinema.

stories.headline: Typhoon Jangmi sweeps northwards leaving 23 injured in Japan
stories.summary: More than 1 million people advised to evacuate homes amid 80mph winds and heavy rainTyphoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) moved northwards over the course of this week. From Okinawa to mainland Japan, prolonged and heavy rainfall led to landslide warnings and the flooding of rivers, with Japan issuing level 4 warnings for some rivers, signalling a risk of overflowing. This level is high enough for municipalities to issue evacuation orders.

stories.headline: The cash-in-the-sofa saga that just won't go away for South Africa's president
stories.summary: A committee of MPs has been formed to look at the case against Cyril Ramaphosa, but he may survive a vote.

stories.headline: Norway's crown princess on lung transplant waiting list, palace says
stories.summary: Mette-Marit's condition, which stiffens the lungs, making it hard to breathe, has deteriorated, the royal household says.

stories.headline: China cracks down on soft porn, violence and materialism in viral micro dramas
stories.summary: Micro dramas have surged in popularity, but drawn criticism for often sensationalist content.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill 11 people in Gaza City, medics say
stories.summary: Women and children were among those killed in the attacks on four residential buildings, which Israel's military says killed four Hamas security officials.

stories.headline: Fifa bans all water bottles from World Cup games
stories.summary: Fifa has been warned it is putting fans' health at risk by introducing a bottle ban at this summer's World Cup.

stories.headline: Protesters topple World Cup player statues in Mexico City
stories.summary: Teachers demanding higher pay have been demonstrating for several days and threaten to disrupt the tournament if their demands are not met.

stories.headline: Miracle on Everest: Guide believed dead spotted crawling down ice
stories.summary: Cleaners found Dawa Sherpa crawling towards Base Camp six days after he went missing at a higher altitude.

stories.headline: Tough cookies: How pop group Le Sserafim overcame band tensions and internet trolls
stories.summary: The K-pop band say accepting their flaws and embracing humour took them to a new level of success.

stories.headline: Bondi Beach shooting hero charged with assault
stories.summary: Ahmed al Ahmed disarmed one of the alleged shooters during the deadly attack at a Jewish event.

stories.headline: Delcy Rodríguez visits India: Will oil talks lead to an energy deal?
stories.summary: Delcy Rodríguez's India visit highlights Venezuela's growing role in Delhi's oil diversification.

stories.headline: Civilians flee as Somali troops and opposition-allied militias trade fire in Mogadishu
stories.summary: Violence flares before protests on Thursday over president’s decision to remain in office after his term expiredFierce clashes have taken place between government troops and militias allied with the opposition in Somalia’s capital, Mogadishu, damaging property and forcing some civilians to flee.In the runup to the fighting, which started on Wednesday afternoon, opposition leaders embedded with militias set up positions in their clan strongholds t

stories.headline: Canada endorses embattled marine park’s plan to relocate 30 beluga whales
stories.summary: Beluga whales, which Marineland threatened to euthanize in 2025, will be moved to aquariums in Spain or across USCanada and an embattled marine park have reached a tentative deal on the future of 30 beluga whales, ending a saga that has captivated the public and angered animal rights groups.The federal fisheries ministry announced this week that all of Marineland’s belugas would be shipped to either Spain or one of four locations in the US, endin

stories.headline: Costly fuel pushes more Indians to buy electric cars but challenges remain
stories.summary: High fuel prices are strengthening the case for EV adoption in the world's third largest auto market.

stories.headline: Canada formally requests 16-year renewal of North American free trade pact
stories.summary: In a letter to his North American counterparts, US-Canada Trade Minister Dominic LeBlanc said the three countries should renew the USMCA trade deal.

stories.headline: Clashes continue in Lebanon despite Israel and Hezbollah accepting US partial ceasefire plan
stories.summary: A cessation of hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah is seen as crucial to the peace process with Iran.

stories.headline: Video shows elaborate drug-smuggling tunnel between US and Mexico
stories.summary: Four people have been arrested and charged with trafficking more than $45 million in cocaine, according to federal officials.

stories.headline: Bowen: Trump needs this war to end but Iran is not backing down
stories.summary: Under pressure from the polls and Gulf allies, the White House is pushing for a deal but Iran wants concessions, writes BBC's international editor.

stories.headline: Israel seizes castle in Lebanon as it expands ground offensive
stories.summary: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu calls the capture of the strategic fortress a "decisive shift" in Israel's campaign against Hezbollah, as European governments criticise the escalation.
